text:
MOLECULAR GENETICS / GERMLINE GENETIC TESTING REPORT

---

PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
DATE OF BIRTH: [on file]
SEX: Male
ORDERING PROVIDER: Medical Oncology
INDICATION: Metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (5+4), BRCA2/HRR germline risk assessment; family history of prostate cancer (father, age 78) and ovarian cancer (paternal aunt). Referred following standard-of-care germline testing guidelines for high-risk/metastatic prostate cancer.
GENETIC COUNSELOR: Genetics and Genomic Medicine, Hereditary Cancer Risk Program

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen ID: [LAB ACCESSION NUMBER ON FILE]
Specimen Type: Peripheral whole blood, EDTA (lavender-top tube)
Specimen Volume Received: 5.0 mL
Specimen Quality: Adequate; extracted DNA quantity and purity within acceptable parameters (A260/A280 = 1.84; yield 4.2 micrograms)
Date of Collection: [on file]
Date Received in Laboratory: [on file]
Date of Report: [on file]
Date of Genetic Counseling Pre-test Session: [on file]

---

TESTING METHODOLOGY

Platform: Comprehensive Germline Hereditary Cancer Panel (84-gene next-generation sequencing panel)
Sequencing Technology: Massively parallel sequencing (Illumina-based NGS) with average coverage depth >500x for all targeted exonic and flanking intronic regions; minimum coverage threshold: 20x at all reportable positions.
Variant Detection: Single nucleotide variants (SNVs), small insertions/deletions (indels), and large copy number variants (CNVs/large deletions/duplications) detected using validated bioinformatic pipelines.
Confirmatory Testing: Sanger sequencing and/or MLPA performed as applicable per laboratory standard operating procedures.
Genes Analyzed: Comprehensive 84-gene panel includes, but is not limited to: APC, ATM, AXIN2, BAP1, BARD1, BLM, BMPR1A, BRCA1, BRCA2, BRIP1, CDH1, CDK4, CDKN2A, CHEK2, CTNNA1, DICER1, EPCAM, FANCC, FANCL, FLCN, FH, GALNT12, GREM1, HOXB13, MLH1, MRE11A, MSH2, MSH6, MUTYH (mono- and bi-allelic), NBN, NF1, NF2, NTHL1, PALB2, PIK3CA, PMS2, PRSS1, PTCH1, PTEN, RAD51B, RAD51C, RAD51D, RB1, RET, RINT1, SDHA, SDHB, SDHC, SDHD, SDHAF2, SMAD4, SMARCA4, SMARCB1, STK11, SUFU, TMEM127, TP53, TSC1, TSC2, VHL, WT1, and others.

---

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

OVERALL RESULT: PATHOGENIC VARIANT IDENTIFIED

Gene: BRCA2
Nomenclature (cDNA): c.5946delT
Nomenclature (Protein): p.Ser1982Argfs*22
Variant Classification: PATHOGENIC
Zygosity: HETEROZYGOUS
Exon: 11
Hereditary Cancer Syndrome: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome (HBOC) / Hereditary Prostate Cancer
Inheritance Pattern: Autosomal dominant
ClinVar Accession: [on file; well-characterized pathogenic variant]
gnomAD Population Frequency: <0.01% (rare)

VARIANT INTERPRETATION:
The identified variant, BRCA2 c.5946delT (p.Ser1982Argfs*22), is a single-nucleotide deletion in exon 11 of the BRCA2 gene, resulting in a frameshift that introduces a premature stop codon 22 amino acids downstream of the alteration (p.Ser1982Argfs*22). This variant is predicted to produce a truncated and/or unstable BRCA2 protein via nonsense-mediated mRNA decay, resulting in loss of function of the BRCA2 protein. BRCA2 encodes a critical tumor suppressor protein involved in homologous recombination-mediated DNA double-strand break repair. Loss of BRCA2 function leads to genomic instability and predisposition to multiple cancer types.

This specific variant (c.5946delT) is a well-characterized, recurrently identified pathogenic variant in the literature and in the ClinVar database, with classification supported by:
  - Evidence of pathogenicity from multiple independent laboratories (ClinVar multiple submitters, consensus classification: Pathogenic)
  - Frameshift nature resulting in premature termination codon (null allele)
  - Co-segregation with disease in affected families reported in the literature
  - Absent or extremely rare frequency in general population databases (gnomAD)
  - Strong prior probability based on variant type and location within a functionally critical domain (OB-fold domain region, critical for RAD51 interaction and HRR activity)

---

ADDITIONAL GERMLINE FINDINGS (REPORTED VARIANTS OF OTHER CLASSIFICATIONS)

The following genes were analyzed with NO pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants identified:
  - ATM: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - CDK12: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - CHEK2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - BRCA1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - PALB2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - HOXB13: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants identified (Lynch syndrome genes, wild-type)
  - TP53: No germline pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - PTEN: No germline pathogenic variant identified (wild-type)
  - All remaining 71 genes on the panel: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants identified.

Variant(s) of Uncertain Significance (VUS): None identified on this panel.

Incidental / Secondary Findings: None meeting ACMG Tier 1 secondary findings criteria identified on this analysis beyond the primary reported finding.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

Confirmatory Sanger Sequencing: Performed for BRCA2 c.5946delT; result CONFIRMED. Heterozygous single-nucleotide deletion at position c.5946 confirmed in independent Sanger sequencing reaction.

MLPA (Multiplex Ligation-dependent Probe Amplification): Performed for BRCA1 and BRCA2 large deletion/duplication analysis. RESULT: No large deletions or duplications detected in BRCA1 or BRCA2.

Quality Metrics: All quality control thresholds met. Internal positive and negative controls performed as expected. Laboratory is CAP-accredited and CLIA-certified. Testing performed in accordance with ACMG Standards and Guidelines for Interpretation and Reporting of Sequence Variants (Richards et al., Genetics in Medicine, 2015).

---

GROSS / PRE-ANALYTICAL DESCRIPTION

Peripheral blood received in EDTA (lavender-top) collection tube, appropriately labeled, accompanied by a completed requisition form. Sample was processed upon receipt per standard laboratory protocol: leukocytes isolated via standard salting-out method (or equivalent validated protocol); genomic DNA extracted, quantified by spectrophotometry and fluorometry. DNA integrity confirmed by agarose gel electrophoresis. Sample met all acceptance criteria. Library preparation performed using standard targeted enrichment approach; sequencing performed on a validated next-generation sequencing instrument platform. All pre-analytical and analytical quality metrics are within laboratory-specified acceptable ranges.

---

CLINICAL INTERPRETATION SUMMARY

This germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22) is identified in the heterozygous state in this patient with metastatic high-grade prostate adenocarcinoma (Gleason 9, Stage IVB). Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variants are associated with a substantially elevated lifetime risk for the following malignancies:
  - Prostate cancer: Estimated cumulative lifetime risk 19-34% (vs. ~12% population risk); associated with higher grade disease, earlier onset, and more aggressive clinical behavior
  - Breast cancer (male): Elevated lifetime risk (~7-8%)
  - Breast cancer (female, in female relatives): Approximately 47-72% lifetime risk
  - Ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal cancer (in female relatives): Approximately 11-17% lifetime risk
  - Pancreatic cancer: Approximately 2-7% lifetime risk (2-6x relative risk above general population)
  - Melanoma (uveal and cutaneous): Modestly elevated risk

First-degree and second-degree relatives of this patient carry a 50% a priori probability of harboring this same heterozygous BRCA2 pathogenic variant. Cascade genetic testing of at-risk relatives is strongly recommended.

With respect to the patient's current cancer diagnosis: The identification of a germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant in the setting of metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer has direct therapeutic implications. BRCA2-mutant prostate cancers demonstrate loss of homologous recombination repair (HRR) capacity, rendering tumors potentially sensitive to PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, rucaparib) upon progression to castration resistance. Somatic tumor testing (e.g., liquid or tissue-based comprehensive genomic profiling) is recommended to evaluate for biallelic inactivation of BRCA2 (second somatic hit/LOH), which would confirm tumor-level HRR deficiency and support PARP inhibitor eligibility per FDA-approved indications.

Genetic counselor note: Patient was seen in pre-test and post-test genetic counseling sessions. Patient was informed of germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant result, hereditary cancer syndrome implications, cancer surveillance recommendations, therapeutic implications in the context of his prostate cancer diagnosis, and the importance of informing at-risk family members. Written educational materials provided. Letter for relatives available upon request. Patient expressed understanding and was receptive to disclosure of results to family members at his discretion.

---

SIGNATURES

Interpreting Laboratory Director: [Molecular Pathology / Molecular Genetics, Board-Certified, ABMGG / ABPath]
Certified Genetic Counselor: [Hereditary Cancer Risk Program, CGC, ABGC-certified]

This report has been reviewed and released by a board-certified laboratory director. This test was developed and its performance characteristics determined by this laboratory. It has not been cleared or approved by the FDA. The FDA has determined that such clearance or approval is not necessary. This laboratory is certified under the Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments of 1988 (CLIA-88) as qualified to perform high-complexity clinical laboratory testing.
